modernization.description: Primary development program including airframe design, stealth technology integration, and systems testing. Multiple prototypes reportedly built and tested.
modernization.impact: Will provide China's first indigenous strategic stealth bomber capability

modernization.description: Development of indigenous turbofan engines to reduce dependence on foreign technology and improve performance characteristics.
modernization.impact: Critical for operational independence and optimal performance parameters

vulnerabilities.description: PLAAF lacks institutional experience operating strategic stealth bombers in contested environments, including mission planning, electronic warfare, and penetration tactics.
vulnerabilities.context: Stealth bomber operations require sophisticated training, doctrine, and support systems that take decades to develop
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Extensive simulation training and gradual capability buildup planned

vulnerabilities.description: Actual radar cross-section and stealth performance against modern integrated air defense systems remains unproven in operational conditions.
vulnerabilities.context: US and allied air defense systems specifically designed to counter stealth threats
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Continuous refinement of stealth technology and electronic warfare capabilities

vulnerabilities.description: Complex manufacturing requirements and advanced materials may limit production rate, reducing fleet size and operational availability.
vulnerabilities.context: China's aerospace industry still developing capabilities for low-rate initial production of advanced stealth aircraft
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Investment in specialized manufacturing facilities and supply chain development

vulnerabilities.description: Long-range missions against distant targets require aerial refueling capabilities that may be vulnerable to enemy action.
vulnerabilities.context: China's tanker fleet is limited and would present high-value targets in conflict
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Development of additional tanker aircraft and alternative basing strategies

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Strategic nuclear deterrence patrols and long-range conventional strike missions
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Independent operation with fighter escort during transit phases
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Platform not yet operational; projected low initial availability due to complexity

peer_comparisons.key_differences: H-20 appears smaller with reduced payload but potentially better stealth characteristics. B-2 has proven operational record and more mature support systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: Can China's H-20 match the proven B-2 Spirit?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Both represent next-generation stealth bombers, but B-21 incorporates more advanced materials and open architecture. H-20 may enter service first.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Next-Gen Stealth Bomber Race: H-20 vs B-21 Raider

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Tu-160 prioritizes speed and payload over stealth, while H-20 emphasizes low-observability. Completely different design philosophies for strategic bombing.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Stealth vs Speed: H-20 and Tu-160 represent opposite approaches to strategic bombing

peer_comparisons.key_differences: H-6K is a conventional bomber requiring fighter escort, while H-20 designed for independent penetrating strikes. Represents generational leap in Chinese capabilities.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: China's Bomber Evolution: From H-6K to H-20 stealth bomber
